test_cases.description: Solitude/remote work podcast — Off-Grid — lighthouse keeper concept, 4 formats, Flux
test_cases.input_summary: Clear core concept, dark editorial style, 4 formats requested (1:1, 16:9, 4:5, 3:1), text overlay accommodation specified, Flux as generation tool
test_cases.output_summary: Shared style anchor extracted and labeled; each format has composition note explaining why it differs from others (not just why the ratio changed); text-safe zones specified per format; generation sequence with seed workflow clearly explained; composition logic is sound (focal point shifts between formats)

test_cases.description: Cooking show — warm, kitchen-focused — YouTube + Instagram only, 2 formats
test_cases.input_summary: Bright warm aesthetic (counter to dark default), 2 formats only (16:9 and 4:5), cooking subject, Instagram food photography register
test_cases.output_summary: Shared anchor correctly shifts to warm register; 16:9 composition uses table-level camera angle for depth; 4:5 moves to closer framing that works for Instagram food photography; composition notes explain how vertical format changes what food imagery emphasizes

test_cases.description: Investigative journalism newsletter — 3 formats, Midjourney, must accommodate heavy text overlay
test_cases.input_summary: Editorial photography style, 3 formats, Midjourney as tool, text overlay is heavy (headline + subhead), news media register
test_cases.output_summary: Prompt grammar adapted for Midjourney (not Flux); text-safe zones specified as large areas because of heavy overlay requirement; compositions designed around the text placement first; limitation about Midjourney vs Flux differences noted in generation sequence

notes: The composition-note-per-format is the most valuable element — explaining why each format is composed differently (not just resized differently) is the core insight of this skill. Actionability is strong because the output is a ready-to-use prompt set with a clear workflow. The seed documentation guidance is essential and often missing from basic prompt tutorials.
